channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Miami says Messi diagnosed with hamstring fatigue ahead of Argentina camp
item.description: Messi’s return to physical activity will “depend on his clinical and functional progress”, according to Inter Miami.

item.title: Taiwan monitors ‘unprovoked’ Chinese combat patrol near island
item.description: Taiwan reports second Chinese 'joint combat readiness patrol' in a week, says its forces responded to the situation.

item.title: Knicks reach NBA Finals after defeating Cavaliers in Game 4
item.description: New York Knicks are heading to the NBA Finals for the first time in 27 years after sweeping Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: Rubio says Strait of Hormuz will open ‘one way or the other’
item.description: Rubio says Strait of Hormuz will open 'one way or the other'

item.title: As Trump woos China, the Quad grouping drifts towards irrelevance
item.description: As Washington shifts military focus away from Asia, analysts say the Quad is struggling to define its purpose.

item.title: ‘Come back, my son’: Indian exam leak leaves trail of death, despair, anger
item.description: More than 2 million aspiring doctors took India’s NEET examination. But the test was compromised and cancelled.

item.title: Rubio says Iran deal could take days after US forces launch new attacks
item.description: US forces say they hit Iranian missile sites and boats as Iranian delegation in Qatar for talks on extending ceasefire.

item.title: Iran war day 88: US attacks near Strait of Hormuz; talks under way in Qatar
item.description: US forces carry out series of attacks in the country's south as Iranian delegation holds talks in Qatar.

item.title: Pakistani women in tent-pegging
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Hala Al Shami explains how Pakistani women are making strides in tent-pegging.

item.title: Starbucks’ Korean sales fall after backlash to ‘Tank Day’ ad campaign
item.description: Coffee chain has seen 'very significant' drop in sales after campaign that evoked crackdown, local operator says.

item.title: US strikes southern Iran in ‘self defence’ targeting Iranian mine boats
item.description: The US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats.

item.title: Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force.

item.title: Istanbul’s Bilgi University reopened after police crackdown
item.description: Students celebrated after days of protests against the closure decree that would have shut Bilgi University mid-year.

item.title: Cost of living crisis reshapes Eid spending in Nigeria
item.description: High costs in Nigeria are reshaping Eid preparations as families adjust spending and cut back on celebrations.

item.title: Hajj pilgrims arrive near Mecca ahead of Arafah Day
item.description: Muslim pilgrims have begun arriving in Mina, near Mecca, ahead of the start of Arafah Day.

item.title: US military launches strikes on southern Iran amid talks in Qatar
item.description: US Central Command announces 'self-defence' strikes as Tehran's top negotiators gather in Qatar for talks.

item.title: Canada’s Mark Carney calls treatment of Gaza flotilla activists ‘appalling’
item.description: The prime minister called for an independent investigation into Israeli mistreatment, but stopped short of penalties.

item.title: Tensions rise in Bolivia as protesters march toward presidential palace
item.description: Demonstrators gathered near the presidential palace in La Paz as police maintained blockades and tensions remained high.

item.title: Iran war live: US drone shot down as tensions surge during peace talks
item.description: Attacks threaten US-Iran peace negotiations as Tehran says US Reaper drone 'downed' over its territory.

item.title: Trump renews petition for White House ballroom, pointing to nearby shooting
item.description: The Trump administration has sought nearly $1bn in taxpayer funds to complete the ballroom project, citing security.

item.title: Czech police detain Russian priest over ‘white substance’ find
item.description: Moscow condemned the actions of the Czech police, calling the detainment a 'provocation'.

item.title: Israel to intensify Lebanon offensive to ‘crush’ Hezbollah
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu's order comes despite 'ceasefire' agreed with Lebanon last month, which was recently extended.

item.title: Bolivia’s president cuts salary in half amid protests
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz says he and his cabinet ministers will cut their salaries by 50 percent.

item.title: Trump pays tribute to US troops killed in war on Iran on Memorial Day
item.description: US President Donald Trump marked Memorial Day at Arlington National Cemetery, paying tribute to US troops killed.

item.title: Ex-Scottish National Party chief pleads guilty to embezzling funds
item.description: Peter Murrell admitted the offences at the High Court in Edinburgh after an investigation into the party's finances.
